Details: A study [2179] using co-precipitation followed by microfiltration showed high removal (99.4%). Although the mean decontamination factor (DF) value was lower for a sodium carbonate dosage of 1000 mg/L than for a dosage of 2000 mg/L, the rate of decline in the specific flux (SF) of the membrane was retarded, and the amount of water treated increased. The concentrations of calcium and magnesium in the water significantly affected the amount of solids rejected by the membranes.
